events.title: Tanits Toon
events.description: New Year Eve inspired by the Armenian tradition of moving from house to house, where the State Philharmonia of Armenia transforms into a galaxy of temporary worlds for one night

events.title: 1915 The Armenian Files & Conspiratio
events.description: A concert and film screening featuring unpublished images shot in Bourj Hammoud (Beirut) presented by Roberto Paci Dalò

events.title: Bring Your Own Beamer
events.description: A one-night-only happening audio-visual jam for visual artists and musicians to showcase work in an immersive format

events.title: NOPA Festival
events.description: Educational program for NOPA festival featuring a three-day hybrid seminar and workshop on sound as spatial phenomenon

events.title: Yerevan Music Week: Dark Jazz Concert
events.venue_name: State Philharmonia of Armenia Ground Floor
events.description: Concert by Mard Mutantar — unfolded as a ritual in sound. A research-driven project born at Notations Lab

events.title: The Other Sounds: Transforming Folk Recordings into Digital Instruments
events.venue_name: State Philharmonia of Armenia Ground Floor
events.description: Workshop by Davit Sukiasyan exploring traditional and folk recordings as raw material for modern sound design

events.title: Audible Spaces
events.venue_name: State Philharmonia of Armenia Ground Floor
events.description: Workshop by Sebastian Frisch exploring human spatial hearing and its creative application in computer music

events.title: Notations Laboratory & Showcase
events.description: A two-week interdisciplinary laboratory and festival where sound, media, and performance meet Armenian classical heritage

events.title: Artificial Intelligence in Music Composition
events.venue_name: State Philharmonia of Armenia Ground Floor
events.description: An introduction to the evolving role of artificial intelligence and algorithmic systems in music composition by Lois Macdonald

events.title: On Sound as Material
events.description: Robert Lippok offers insight into his artistic approach to sound as both a sculptural and architectural element

events.title: Design Stories
events.description: A lecture connecting with the Armenian international design scene through stories behind Maria Cristina Didero’s projects

events.title: Basic Sound Synthesis #2
events.description: Lecture on sound as a way of thinking through emotion, structure, and atmosphere by Shahen Khandkaryan (aka Shhau)

events.title: The Role of Project Thinking in the Creative Process
events.short_title: Project Thinking in the Creative Process
events.description: Open lecture on where inspiration meets structure and how project thinking operates inside the creative process by Aram Hovhannisyan

partners.name: State Philharmonia of Armenia
partners.fields: Music, Performance, Art Education, Festivals & Events, Cultural Heritage, Community & Youth
partners.description: Throughout its activity, Philharmonia has played a significant role in preserving and developing Armenian musical culture. International educational and cognitive projects, concerts, festivals, competitions, master classes, presentations are organised by the institution. Philharmonia is also engaged in the production of scientific-cognitive films and publishing. The mission of Philharmonia is to contribute to the development of the country's cultural sector by presenting the best works of Armenian and world art.

partners.name: Gabriel Sundukyan National Academic Theatre
partners.description: The Gabriel Sundukyan State Academic Theatre, founded in 1922, is the oldest modern theatre in Armenia.

partners.name: Common Ground
partners.type: key actors of the field in Armenia, center
partners.description: Common Ground is a special place in the heart of Yerevan - a cultural space where one can find like-minded people to connect with and make cultural exchange. Common Ground is also a home for various cultural events: lectures, presentations, movie screenings, concerts etc.

partners.name: WOODS
partners.type: key actors of the field in Armenia, center
partners.description:
The way WOODS experience gatherings today isn’t just about finding a venue – it’s about creating something meaningful.

At Woods, we go beyond the ordinary to offer more than just an event space – we invite you into a setting where every gathering becomes part of a larger story.

partners.name: Rambalkoshe
partners.type: key actors of the field in Armenia, center
partners.description: Rambalkoshe isn't merely a contemporary art museum; it's a vibrant hub of creativity and collaboration driven by a family of individuals who are passionate about unearthing emotions through sound and shape. In essence, Rambalkoshe is a testament to the power of art and community, where every moment invites you to embrace the beauty of the human spirit.

partners.name: NOPA
partners.type: key actors of the field in Armenia, festival
partners.description: NOPA unites more than 30 participants including artists, musicians and composers from Armenia, Georgia, Germany, France, Switzerland, Netherlands, Brazil, Italy, Argentina, Turkey, who will collaborate and interact closely with the spaces to create a whole new sonic experience for the audience.
